Soulection Radio Show #193 w/ SoSuperSam, Hannah Faith, Sasha Marie, Yukibeb, Kronika, & Eden
Soulection Radio Show #158 w/ Kronika
show.content:
Joe Kay & Kronika hold it down with gems randing from unreleased to forthcoming material, plus forgotten classics. 

<!--more-->
Here is our last the last show of 2013 and ending it with a BANG. 

Every week just keeps getting better and better for us. The music, the accomplishments, and ideas just keep rising. 

This week was supposed to be a ladies edition of Soulection Radio w/ Sasha Marie, Eden, and Kronika but Sasha and Eden both couldn't make it out this time along. 

We'll catch you in 2014 and thank you again for your support. We appreciate you.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #154
show.content:
Joe Kay really displayed a crazy selection of music that will have you copying/pasting consistently within Google for some time. 

<!--more-->

There are a lot of new tunes sprinkled with the 'feel-good' classics that we always connect to our sets. It's essential for us to keep educating you with the influences and how it all connects to where we are today. Spread this set to as many people as possible! More minds and ears need to hear this music!

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #150 w/ SOSUPERSAM
show.content:
Talented & vibrant woman SOSUPERSAM finally came through the studio. 

<!--more-->
It's been a long time coming and we're happy that you finally get a feel for who she is musically and as a person. Wasn't quite sure what she was gonna play for us, but she showed a great diversity and really brought us back to a place that was very much needed towards the end of her set. She's even cooler as a person. Being humble and driven will always get you far. Joe Kay really brought out a diverse selection of music. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #164 w/ Jamil & Tunji
show.content:
3 Hours of 'just music' with friends Jamil & Tunji.
<!--more-->

This Soulection Radio session is one for the books! We wanted to switch it up on you, put the full 3 hours of 'just music' aside and inform you with some real knowledge from some great friends of ours who are some very important people that you need to know. Jamil who is Drake's touring manager and G-Eazy's co-manager is someone who has reached out to us and showed us so much support. Tunji on the other hand is an army swiss knife! He is an A&R at Interscope and is one of the most humble and diverse A&R's in the game. One of the few that truly understands the sound of tomorrow and is responsible for some amazing connections going on in the industry. Really listen to what they have to say and take notes!

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #168 (SXSW Pre Show)
show.content:
Joe kay getting you ready for SXSW.
<!--more-->

As we are getting ready for SXSW we wanted to get you in the spirit. Joe Kay went solo again to keep things simple and focused. He kicked it off with the new Carmack/Esta collab, played some new discoveries/unreleased gems, and then switched up the whole format by letting the live listener selects the music by sending us our requests via our social media networks. What a beautiful thing! For over 2 hours, you had the chance to selects the music, Joe was just the provider! What a great way to collaborate right? Since we will be at SXSW from (March 11-16) there won't be a live Soulection Radio show next week, however, we do have a special guest mix to make up for it. Stay tuned.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #169
show.content:
Plenty of new music stacked ready to share with you.
<!--more-->

It felt good to be back after being away from the studio for 4 weeks. We've been traveling for the past month spreading The Sound of Tomorrow at SXSW, WMC, and now Asia. The pros of being away for so long meant that Joe stacked up on plenty of music for the past month which makes up for the lost time, trust. There is too much to say and talk about but shout out to all of the amazing people who came out to our shows to see and connect with us. We are very grateful!

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

data.title: Untitled (How Does it Feel) (L-Vis 1990's Own Way Edit)

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #140 w/ Esta & Andres
show.content:
ESTA and Andres stop on by and deliver solid sets.

<!--more-->

Had to start this show with a new unreleased gem from Kaytra and play all the classics/unreleased material as usual. Esta finally stopped by to drop an official set and talk about what's next for him. He really impressed us with his house vibes. This man is diverse. Andres also came through and graced us with a heavy hitter set. Spread this if you feel it.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #171
show.content:
Joe Kay returned with an immense amount and plenty of new ideas/inspiration after Coachella Weekend 1. 

<!--more-->

There are so many new gems in here. We also recently discovered this new platform/label out of Sydney called: [@flowfi](https://soundcloud.com/flowfi) (Accidentally spelled them out wrong on the show as F-L-O), but these guys are coming with HEAT. Make sure you pay attention to them. If you feel this whole session please share. Lets keep educating people with real music. Catch you next week at the same time.

Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #152 w/ LIKE (Pac Div)
show.content:
Due to all the adversity we went through with this show we had [LIKE](https://soundcloud.com/callmelike) play music past the last hour and extend all the way for a total of 3 hours & 39 minutes. 
<!--more-->

From the very first minute to the very end we were providing music that you never heard of or forgot about, configuring wires, figuring out audio issues, chopping it up about food, you name it. So much effort went into producing this show. We want to thank our brother LIKE for rolling through and blessing us with a proper DJ set, freestyle over Esta's beat, and talking to us about his experiences growing up and being in Pac Div. This man has traveled the world, played alongside the best in the game, and has also produced for/with the best (Kendrick, The Neptunes, ect). Joe Kay made sure to touch on topics that are very insightful. LIKE will be releasing with us on Soulection so stay tuned! 

Follow LIKE on [Twitter](https://twitter.com/Callmelike) | [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/clearlyitslike/) | [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/callmeLike/)


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Boilerroom - Joe Kay 4 hour Streaming from Isolation #14
show.content:
Joe Kay appears on BoilerRoom Live Streaming from Isolation sessions.

<!--more-->


[Please support here if you can.](https://business.facebook.com/donate/219168332618496/)

During the pandemic, tune into our new live series Streaming From Isolation. These broadcasts will stream from artists’ homes and private spaces, direct to our audience and without the need for interaction with production teams - as we practice social distancing to remove risk from our programming.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #369 w/Yukibeb
show.content:
Joe Kay meets up with Japanese DJ [Yukibeb](https://twitter.com/yukibeb) drops a 60-minutes guest mix live in Tokyo. --- Today we're introducing the Black Label Series, which is focused on highlighting rising vocalists/rappers we're going to be working with. As smooth as they are soulful, LA-based group Hablot Brown gives us 5 original tracks that will grab you by the heartstrings. From the vocals to guitar-infused production, the first ever Black Label release will make it hard for you to not follow their journey. Discover:
[soundcloud.com/hablotbrown](https://soundcloud.com/hablotbrown)
[twitter.com/hablotbrown](https://twitter.com/hablotbrown)
[instagram.com/hablotbrown/](https://instagram.com/hablotbrown/) ### Credits Released July 29th, 2018 Mixed & Mastered by Joshua Pleeter & Austin Brown Artwork: Andre Power

Soulection All Day 2024
show.content:
**🎊🌟 Happy New Year 2024 🌟🎊**

The 6th year in a row Soulection Takesover Apple Music with exclusive mixes from Joe Kay, Savvv, Dave Nunes, and Franchise.

<!--more-->

### Jump to:

* [Joe Kay](#joe-kays-set)
* [Savvv](#savvvs-set)
* [Dave Nunes](#dave-nuness-set)
* [Franchise](#franchises-set)

Also listen to the [Soulection All Day 2023](/episodes/all-day-2023), [Soulection All Day 2022](/episodes/all-day-2022), [Soulection All Day 2021](/episodes/all-day-2021), [Soulection All Day 2020](/episodes/all-day-2020), and [Soulection All Day 2019](/episodes/all-day-2019).

show.title: Boilerroom - Joe Kay 4 hour Streaming from Isolation #14
show.content:
Joe Kay appears on BoilerRoom Live Streaming from Isolation sessions.

<!--more-->


[Please support here if you can.](https://business.facebook.com/donate/219168332618496/)

During the pandemic, tune into our new live series Streaming From Isolation. These broadcasts will stream from artists’ homes and private spaces, direct to our audience and without the need for interaction with production teams - as we practice social distancing to remove risk from our programming.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #137 w/ Mr. Carmack & Mikos Da Gawd
show.content:
Mikos Da Gawd and Mr. Carmack each drop amazing sets of unreleased, original productions.

<!--more-->

Joe Kay kicked things off for the first half of the show! He dropped a lot of exclusives and forgotten gems. Mikos Da Gawd dropped a 30+ minute DJ set that consisted of many gems and original productions from the man himself. Mr. Carmack wrapped up the show with an amazing listening session of all of his latest unreleased works. Man, all we gotta say is that Carmack is one of the best in the game right now. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #146
show.content:
Joe Kay hits hard with 3 hours of HEAVY tracks on 1 hour of sleep

<!--more-->


There is something for everybody in here. Lots of unreleased music + music that was forgotten about. Shouts to all the amazing people who tuned in to the live show. Every once in a while we like to make a post about the location of our radio listeners and it's always inspiring to see how many people are actually tuning in and the locations that they reside in. We can't even pronounce some of the cities/countries! Talk about worldwide. Lets keep it moving.


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #148
show.content:
Continuing Joe Kay's 24th birthday celibration with precise selections that you either forgot about or never heard.

<!--more-->

In celebration of Joe's 24th birthday yesterday, we started celebrating on Thursday at The Sound of Tomorrow (The Crosby) with Astronautica & starRo! Thank you to everyone who came out and supported, what a show it was. This radio session is just a continuation of the celebration with precise selections that you either forgot about or never heard. 
Bless to those who tuned into the live broadcast via Kbeach. It's amazing to think about how far we've came. 


Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.

show.title: Soulection Radio Show #202 w/ AbJo, StarRo, & Andre Power (4th Anniversary Edition)
Soulection Radio Show #149 w/ House Shoes
show.content:
The pioneer/legendary House Shoes kicks off exclusives from label Street Corner Music.

<!--more-->

This man's musical selections, experiences/knowledge, and blunt personality is one that you can't deny. 

We respect House Shoes! It was nice to finally have him come by and drop two different sets (that's right two!). 

He kicked it off with exclusives from his new label Street Corner Music and then ended with some nice soulful joints. 

Joe Kay's discussion with Shoes also touched on many great subjects that many of you wanted to know. Enjoy.



Thanks to [true listener^^](https://soundcloud.com/true_listener) for supplying this tracklist.
